title: Missing net_pkt_cursor_init error check
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:292

net_pkt_cursor_init() is called but its return value is not checked. The function can fail if pkt is NULL or malformed.

What goes wrong: If cursor_init fails silently, the subsequent net_pkt_read() operates on an uninitialized cursor, potentially reading garbage or crashing.

Note: Looking at Zephyr source, net_pkt_cursor_init() returns void in current versions, so this may be a non-issue depending on Zephyr version. Verify against target Zephyr version.
close_reason: False positive: net_pkt_cursor_init() returns void per Zephyr API
